text:
Market today: The tech and chip sector faces a tug-of-war. Nvidia's new AI model offers a bullish push, but a stark warning about an impending "Lehman Bros. moment" for the AI bubble casts a long shadow over tech stocks.

Your holdings: SOXL (-34.95%) and SPAL (-37.57%) are your main concerns. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning their current deep drawdowns are amplified, especially on margin. SPAL is also a brand-new, very volatile IPO.

Watch: Keep a close eye on market sentiment around the AI bubble narrative. Any further negative news could hit your concentrated tech book hard. Also, watch for follow-through on Nvidia's AI developments.

Bottom line: Risk management, especially on your leveraged and deeply underwater positions, is paramount right now.
plays: Consider trimming or exiting SOXL (-34.95%) due to its SELL status, leveraged decay, and significant loss. Respect its default stop at 200.
Re-evaluate SPAL (-37.57%), a new leveraged ETF already deep in the red. Its volatility and current loss warrant attention; consider its stop at 21.38.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The AI bubble warning and "chasing" scanner setups reduce conviction for adding to tech.
